notes:
After Update 2.01, EAC (Easy Anti-Cheat) has been updated to allow for Proton to work. The only issue I had was installing the VC redistributables, but that was easily fixed by removing "UE3redists_vs2010.exe" and "UE3redists_vs2012.exe" in the "Binaries/Redist" folder, and then the game ran with no issues. It's advisable to Disable Esync to reduce stuttering of the game.

Also Horses seen in spectate mode jolt around weirdly when people are riding them. But that hardly matters.

Frame Rate (after DXVK shader caching) was around 90- 120 FPS with everything set to maximum at 1080p, about 50 FPS less than on Windows. It is very much playable, fully recommended.
title: Paladins®

notes:
As others have reported, EAC appears to have destroyed this game's online functionality. I opened a support case with EAC and they claimed that they are working with Valve to enable full compatibility with Proton titles. They provided no ETA on this.

Here is the email they sent me:
"Thanks for reaching out!

While Easy Anti-Cheat already supports native Linux games, unfortunately it is not compatible with the Steam Play emulation yet. We are currently working with Valve to bring the support for Steam Play as well. However, at this time we cannot promise a release date.

Our apologies for the inconvenience. Despite the troubles, hope you have a great day."
title: Paladins®

notes:
Runs perfectly at max FPS, 4K all max settings. Online match works very well. Had to install DotNet 4.0 runtime though. If you are using the Steam Native runtime for your distro, I'd suggest switching to Steam's own runtime. 

This is how I got Paladins to work:

» Install protontricks (available in AUR) or get it from GitHub. 
» Run Steam in Native mode at least once (via shell with "STEAM_RUNTIME=1 steam"). This will unpack the native runtimes and launch Steam. You can exit Steam.
» Run "protontricks 444090 dotnet40" in the shell. This will install DotNet 4.0 runtime. Let the installer complete.
» Run Steam as you normally would and launch Paladins. The installer will complain about Windows XP SP3 but you can ignore that. The game will start and run!
» Enjoy!
title: Paladins®

notes: Paladins works again after the 2019-03-28 patch. Played several games online without EAC problems. A friend of mine used Proton 4.2.x to play with me which worked as well. Occasionally, after a match you might get a black screen for like 20 seconds, but this problem predates the EAC issues. Just wait and you will be thrown back to the game with glitched labels. After that you have to click the reconnect button and you'll be able to play again.
title: Paladins®

notes:
I am very confused, as the last two months in this game's section is a solid BORKED wall. For me the game works. I can log in, set borderless window, can tweak loadouts, run through the interface, test champions on the shooting range, play with players against bots and finally play with players against players, it all works (botvp and pvp around 4-5 games summed up). Just did not try ranked games, cause I haven't gotten to that overall yet. The game is playable overall, but once again I probably have to blame my CPU for being ancient here, so protoning the game eats up some FPS. My native Windows (8) is around 70 I think, here it is half slower, with drops to 19 which is pretty much right above the red line which borders a somehow comfortable gameplay. But overall - it works and deserves a Silver from me I think.
Also worth to mention the tweaks I tried. PROTON_USE_WINED3D made the game wreck on start, but PROTON_NO_ESYNC might have helped (but I admit I did not exactly test that reverting back to no esync disabled), cause originally using skills for the first time during the match was causing short, but hard framerate drops. The other time I attempted playing with no esync the drops were gone. Take it as a possible yet not confirmed thing to improve performance in the beginning of the match.

notes:
I'm a long time player of this game on windows 7, I have over 2000 hours in the game. I average 175-120 fps on windows 7 and a few months back the status of the game was much better suited to run on Linux where I averaged around the same fps that I got on windows. Now though I get a huge fluctuation in fps all the way down to 20 with everything on low and even at 720p. Input lag is also so much higher than on windows where I average about 6ms, in proton I average 12-30 (press f9 in game its the third value in the list with "ms" next to the number). This isn't the fault of proton I'd say, its the fault of the developers. Even on windows 7 my fps can drop to like 40 out of no where. The game is just poorly optimized and your experience will vary wildly based on your hardware.

This isn't even mentioning the bugs, the game will crash, fail to login, not allow you to queue with friends in a party, give you deserter penalties for bugging out, and so much more. These problems are all prevalent on windows as well as in proton.
title: Paladins®

notes: tl;dr; currently running smoothly at max settings, 165fps! Method was kinda described in some other posts here, but I used protontricks to install 'dotnet40' however the trick was to use protontricks to configure wine to run as windows 7 instead of windows XP before install dotnet40. After this, whenever I run the game from steam I get 2 error messages about needing at least XP Service Pack 3 (just ignore these) but then the game launches and runs perfectly!
title: Paladins®

notes: Run the game first so that it installs all the stuff and after that do "protontricks 444090 dotnet40" and the game will then launch. Online mode works again (EasyAntiCheat).
title: Paladins®

notes:
Well, as you can see , my PC sucks , so , with the habitual hit in performance you get by running games via Proton , i was able to play the game but not with the settings I used on windows (on windows it runs at max without a hassle , paladins is a well optimized game) but on Ubuntu , using medium settings i was struggling to get 50 fps , but nonetheless , if you have an excellent computer or , even , like a core i3 and a 1050 you could run it flawlessly , i just recommend you to install protontricks (you can find this on github) and , after launching the game once so proton sets up an environment for it , to input the command "protontricks 444090 dotnet40" so it gathers the .NET dependencies.After that , i suggest you to go to your launch options , to type noesync and nod3d11 so it disables both and , once you're in the game , to disable D3D11 and restart the game .
Sorry if it was a bit long but once you've done this , the game will run fine depending on your config. Hope that helps
title: Paladins®
